summary.brief: Flores v. Liu. Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: In July 2011, plaintiff consulted with Dr. Carson Liu (Dr. Liu), a leading and experienced bariatric surgeon, about surgeries that might assist her with her weight loss efforts.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On August 15, 2011, Dr. Liu performed the gastric lap band surgery on plaintiff.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On August 29, 2014, Dr. Liu removed the lap band and performed the gastric sleeve surgery on plaintiff.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On August 10, 2015, Dr. Liu performed the gastric re-sleeve surgery on plaintiff. The surgery was performed competently.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The day after the surgery, one of the staple lines leaked material from plaintiff’s gastroesophageal junction into plaintiff’s abdominal cavity, which caused sepsis, respiratory failure, and acute renal failure.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Plaintiff’s expert was a bariatric surgeon. He opined that Dr. Liu was negligent for recommending gastric re-sleeve surgery for plaintiff.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.text: Dr. Liu’s expert was also a bariatric surgeon. He opined that Dr. Liu acted reasonably in recommending the gastric re-sleeve surgery for plaintiff.
evidence_index.speaker: Defendant's expert

evidence_index.text: The judgment is affirmed. The parties are to bear their own costs on appeal.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: A physician may be liable for negligently recommending a course of treatment if (1) that course stems from a misdiagnosis of the patient’s underlying medical condition, or (2) all reasonable physicians in the relevant medical community would agree that the probable risks of that treatment outweigh its probable benefits.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

evidence_index.text: The trial court should not have submitted plaintiff’s negligent recommendation theory to the jury because the evidence, viewed in the light most favorable to plaintiff, does not support that Dr. Liu was negligent in recommending that plaintiff undergo the gastric re-sleeve surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient has a history of failed weight loss attempts through diet alone.

focused_exam.general: Obese female, 5 feet 2 inches tall, weighing 315 pounds

requestables.name: Psychological Evaluation
requestables.description: Consultation with a psychologist to assess psychological factors contributing to obesity

reveal.result_summary: No psychological issues contributing to obesity

requestables.name: Nutritional Evaluation
requestables.description: Consultation with a nutritionist to assess dietary habits

requestables.name: Swallow Test
requestables.description: Imaging to assess anatomical changes post-surgery

reveal.result_summary: Anatomic failure of the sleeve, stomach stretched

timeline_phases.name: Initial Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Patient consults with Dr. Liu for weight loss surgery options

timeline_phases.name: Pre-Surgical Workup
timeline_phases.description: Patient undergoes evaluations and decides on surgery

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient considering further surgical intervention for weight loss
decision_points.prompt: Should a multi-disciplinary work-up be conducted before recommending gastric re-sleeve surgery?

options.clinical_reasoning: Majority of bariatric surgeons would conduct a new work-up

options.description: Proceed with gastric re-sleeve surgery without new work-up

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with surgery without new work-up
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed previous consultations were sufficient

expected_action_court.description: A new multi-disciplinary work-up should have been conducted
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated majority would conduct new work-up

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony that a new work-up was standard
explanation.what_should_have_happened: A new work-up would have provided updated insights
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Post-surgical complications including staple line leak

key_findings: No substantial evidence that further work-up would have changed the outcome
Court found no negligence in the recommendation of surgery

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Recommendation of surgery without further work-up
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Not established that further work-up would have altered the decision

allegations.allegation: Negligent recommendation of gastric re-sleeve surgery
Negligent recommendation of gastric re-sleeve surgery
